description: The audit package should be installed.
rationale: The auditd service is an access monitoring and accounting daemon, watching system calls to audit any access, in comparison with potential local access control policy such as SELinux policy.
ocil_clause: the audit package is not installed
ocil:
Run the following command to determine if the audit package is installed: 
$ rpm -q audit
fixtext:
Install the audit service (if the audit service is not already installed) with the following command:


$ sudo dnf install audit
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must have the audit package installed.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 audit package must be installed.
stig.vuldiscussion:
Without establishing what type of events occurred, the source of events, where events occurred, and the outcome of events, it would be difficult to establish, correlate, and investigate the events leading up to an outage or attack.

Audit record content that may be necessary to satisfy this requirement includes, for example, time stamps, source and destination addresses, user/process identifiers, event descriptions, success/fail indications, filenames involved, and access control or flow control rules invoked.

Associating event types with detected events in audit logs provides a means of investigating an attack, recognizing resource utilization or capacity thresholds, or identifying an improperly configured Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 system.
stig.checktext:
Verify that the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 audit service package is installed.

Check that the audit service package is installed with the following command:

$ dnf list --installed audit

Example output:

audit-3.0.7-101.el9_0.2.x86_64

If the "audit" package is not installed, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Install the audit service package (if the audit service is not already installed) with the following command:

$ sudo dnf install audit

title: Ensure the audit Subsystem is Installed
